2012-11-01.title: Market Rises, Perfume and All, as Refugees Face a Long Syria War
2012-11-01.abstract: Along the main road through the Zaatari camp in Jordan, there are at least three coffee shops, a brand-new falafel stand inside a tent, and stands selling fruits and vegetables.

2012-11-01.title: In This Corner, a Much-Needed Distraction
2012-11-01.abstract: When a German promoter this week brought the spectacle of professional boxing to Kabul, the only real question for many Afghans was what took so long.

2012-11-01.title: Wary of Future, Professionals Leave China in Record Numbers
2012-11-01.abstract: Although China’s economic boom has created millions of well-paying jobs, its skilled workers are moving elsewhere, in search of better quality of life, and religious and political freedoms.

2012-11-01.title: Russians See Church and State Come Closer
2012-11-01.abstract: The rising prominence of Archimandrite Tikhon Shevkunov, a monk said to be President Vladimir V. Putin’s spiritual adviser, raises questions about the Orthodox Church’s growing power.

2012-11-01.title: Libya Parliament Approves New Premier’s Cabinet
2012-11-01.abstract: The approval of the cabinet brings to an end months of uncertainty about who was in charge of the government.

2012-11-02.title: China: Cafe Owner Sentenced Over Online Messages
2012-11-02.abstract: The owner of an Internet cafe in southwest China was given an eight-year prison term for criticizing the ruling Communists in online messages.

2012-11-02.title: Grabs for Power Behind Plan to Shrink Elite Circle
2012-11-02.abstract: A proposal by Chinese leaders to downsize the powerful Politburo Standing Committee offers one of the clearest windows available into the intentions of the Communist Party.

2012-11-02.title: Video Is Said to Show Syrian Rebels Executing Prisoners
2012-11-02.abstract: The execution of a group of prisoners, apparently soldiers or militiamen, appears to be evidence of a war crime by the rebels, human rights activists said.

2012-11-02.title: A Bird Skeleton, a Code and, Maybe, a Top Secret
2012-11-02.abstract: British officials are trying to break the World War II-era code found on the remains of a carrier pigeon at a home in Surrey.

2012-11-02.title: Leader Ousted, Nation Is Now a Drug Haven
2012-11-02.abstract: Guinea-Bissau, the West African country taken over by its military in April, now appears to be a place where drug trafficking is approved at the top, drug trade experts say.

2012-11-03.title: A Shanghai That Didn’t Last
2012-11-03.abstract: For several years, a correspondent-turned-photographer documented the buildings, the neighborhoods and the way of life that disappeared to make way for a landscape of high-rise buildings that is the new Shanghai. There was no room for these communities in the image that the city envisioned for itself.

2012-11-03.title: Iran Sanctions Take Unexpected Toll on Medical Imports
2012-11-03.abstract: A ban on financial transactions with Iran has inadvertently affected critical supplies that are exempted from Western sanctions.

2012-11-03.title: Chinese Patrol Ships Pressuring Japan Over Islands
2012-11-03.abstract: Analysts say the 14th straight day of patrols in the East China Sea suggests China is trying to wear down Japan’s resolve.

2012-11-03.title: Nigerian Forces Kill Dozens in Night Assault, Fueling Long Battle With Sect
2012-11-03.abstract: Security forces in the city of Maiduguri shot dead dozens of young men whom they accused of belonging to the radical Islamic sect Boko Haram.

2012-11-03.title: Peeking Through Years, and the Wall, at Oswald
2012-11-03.abstract: K.G.B. files, as well as recent and coming books by researchers and people who knew Lee Harvey Oswald, provide an intimate look at his time in Minsk, Belarus.

2012-11-04.title: Libya Attack Shows Pentagon’s Limits in Region
2012-11-04.abstract: The Pentagon’s Africa Command, based in Germany, lacked a quick-response force to send to Libya when American diplomatic posts came under attack in Benghazi.

2012-11-04.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-11-04.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.

2012-11-04.title: The Brazilian Economic Model
2012-11-04.abstract: While Europe, the United States and China engage in ideological fights, Brazil’s Dilma Rousseff reaches for a centrist economics.

2012-11-04.title: Why Socialist Europe Is Better for Families than America
2012-11-04.abstract: Writing in Slate, an American expatriate mom makes the pocketbook case for how Romney and the Republicans hurt middle-class families. (And Obama and the Democrats don’t necessarily help.)

2012-11-04.title: China Party Endorses Expulsion in Scandal
2012-11-04.abstract: The party’s Central Committee endorsed the expulsion of Bo Xilai, who had been a contender for one of the party’s highest positions until his spectacular fall this year.

2012-11-05.title: Officials at G-20 Meeting Warn About Debt in Europe and U.S.
2012-11-05.abstract: Finance ministers and central bank governors met amid mounting alarm that the euro-zone crisis and Washington’s failure to deal with soaring deficits could endanger the fragile global recovery.

2012-11-05.title: Clashes and Car Bombing Highlight Insecurity Across Libya
2012-11-05.abstract: Armed with guns and rocket-propelled grenades, rival Libyan militias fought on the streets of Tripoli, Libyan officials said, highlighting the country’s persisting lawlessness and insecurity.

2012-11-05.title: As NATO Nears Exit, Construction Dries Up
2012-11-05.abstract: The impending American and NATO withdrawal has not only slashed military spending, but also dramatically reduced construction projects, stopping a building boom in its tracks.

2012-11-05.title: Palestinian’s Remark, Seen as Concession, Stirs Uproar
2012-11-05.abstract: President Mahmoud Abbas jolted Palestinians and Israelis over the right-of-return issue by saying he would not want to move back to his birth city, Safed, which is now part of Israel.

2012-11-05.title: The Nehru-Gandhi Dynasty
2012-11-05.abstract: For most of modern India’s history, being a member of the Nehru-Gandhi political family was enough to win votes and public confidence. But the clan’s mystique has been jeopardized by rapid change.

2012-11-06.title: The Vatican: Leaks Trial, Part II
2012-11-06.abstract: The Vatican computer expert accused of aiding and abetting the theft of the pope’s confidential papers began his trial on Monday.

2012-11-06.title: Has Hong Kong Reached Its ‘Tipping Point’ as a World City?
2012-11-06.abstract: Hong Kong’s standing as a key Asian location for foreign firms is under threat, analysts say, due to skyrocketing rents and a shortage of commercial and residential space.

2012-11-06.title: Iran: Canadian Ruling Assailed
2012-11-06.abstract: Relations between Iran and Canada, already at a low point, worsened Monday as Iran denounced a court ruling that froze Iranian government assets in Canada.

2012-11-06.title: Han Suyin Dies; Wrote Sweeping Fiction
2012-11-06.abstract: Dr. Han, who was believed to have been 96, was a medical doctor and proponent of Mao who also wrote novels and memoirs, including one that became the Hollywood film “Love Is a Many-Splendored Thing.”

2012-11-06.title: An Industry Struggles to Keep Its Luster
2012-11-06.abstract: Antwerp, where nearly 85 percent of the world’s uncut diamonds are traded, is fighting to maintain its role as a home for cutting, polishing and selling the gems.

2012-11-07.title: A Rare Visitor Finds a Home With Gazans
2012-11-07.abstract: Crocodiles are not native to Gaza, but that did not stop one from living on the loose for two years in a sewage tunnel.

2012-11-07.title: Egypt Reacts to Presidential Election
2012-11-07.abstract: On the eve of the United States presidential election, Egyptians were skeptical about both President Obama and Mitt Romney.

2012-11-07.title: High Ambition and Visions of Andean Haute Cuisine
2012-11-07.abstract: The Danish celebrity chef Claus Meyer is opening a restaurant in La Paz, hoping to start a Bolivian food movement that will rediscover local ingredients.

2012-11-07.title: Mayor of Montreal Resigns as Corruption Investigation Heats Up
2012-11-07.abstract: Mayor Gérald Tremblay resigned Monday in the midst of an inquiry that has revealed widespread corruption among city officials, contractors and members of organized crime.

2012-11-07.title: In New Delhi, a Breeding Ground for Dengue
2012-11-07.abstract: India has become a focal point for the plague that is sweeping the globe.

2012-11-08.title: Netanyahu Rushes to Repair Damage With Obama
2012-11-08.abstract: The results of Tuesday’s presidential election left many Israelis questioning whether Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had risked their collective relationship with Washington.

2012-11-08.title: Strays Amid Rome Ruins Set Off a Culture Clash
2012-11-08.abstract: Italy’s state archaeologists have told a cat shelter it has to go, saying the cats risk damaging a fragile ancient monument, but the shelter is a popular tourist draw.

2012-11-08.title: Long Retired, Ex-Leader of China Asserts Sway Over Top Posts
2012-11-08.abstract: Factions in the Chinese leadership had lacked a dominant elder statesman like Jiang Zemin to arbitrate disputes.

2012-11-08.title: Changing of the Guard in China
2012-11-08.abstract: The New York Times’s Beijing correspondents discuss the challenges ahead for China as the country begins its once-in-a-decade leadership transition.

2012-11-08.title: What a Darkened New York Revealed to Me
2012-11-08.abstract: Being alone and independent works when things are good, and even when things are tough, but this time was different, it cut closer to the bone.

2012-11-09.title: Does Dotcom Have New Zealand Wired? Not Yet.
2012-11-09.abstract: The famous — and infamous — millionaire Internet impresario has caused another stir in his adopted land, and this time it does not seem to be illegal.

2012-11-09.title: For Seniors, a College Checklist for November
2012-11-09.abstract: Two college counselors provide advice and offer next steps to high school seniors who are either “not there yet” or “in the waiting room” of the college admissions process.

2012-11-09.title: In Big New Museum, Russia Has a Message for Jews: We Like You
2012-11-09.abstract: A large, high-tech Jewish history museum, personally championed by Vladimir V. Putin and underwritten by oligarchs, opened in Moscow on Thursday.

2012-11-09.title: Ukraine’s Ultranationalists Show Surprising Strength at Polls
2012-11-09.abstract: The unexpectedly strong showing of the Svoboda party in recent parliamentary voting stirs concerns about the rise of xenophobia and anti-Semitism in Ukraine.

2012-11-09.title: Student Killed Amid Protest in Dominican Republic
2012-11-09.abstract: A man was shot at the country’s largest public university in a clash between the police and protesters angry about a law that will raise sales taxes and a proposal to privatize the school.

2012-11-10.title: From Indonesia, a Documentary With Killers as the Stars
2012-11-10.abstract: “The Act of Killing” allows Indonesian gangsters to re-create their killings of communists in 1965, purges left as many as 3 million dead. The gangsters, lionized by many Indonesians, used sets, costumes, makeup and even a song-and-dance number to recount their deeds.

2012-11-10.title: Life in Prison Suite Doesn’t Agree With a Mass Killer
2012-11-10.abstract: Anders Behring Breivik, who killed 77 people in Norway in 2011, complained about being strip-searched, isolated and being issued a pen that made his hand cramp.

2012-11-10.title: A Vague Role for Religion in Egyptian Draft Constitution
2012-11-10.abstract: A compromise in the constituent assembly accepted that “the principles of Islamic law” should guide law, but leaves it up to Parliament and the courts to determine how and what.

2012-11-10.title: Canada: Another Mayor Resigns
2012-11-10.abstract: Gilles Vaillancourt, the mayor of Laval, Quebec, for 23 years, resigned Friday, becoming the second municipal leader in the province to quit this week amid accusations of corruption.

2012-11-10.title: Climate Change Report Outlines Perils for U.S. Military
2012-11-10.abstract: A study commissioned by the C.I.A. and other agencies says that the acceleration of climate change will place unparalleled strains on the American military and intelligence agencies in coming years.

2012-11-11.title: Off Script at China’s 18th Party Congress
2012-11-11.abstract: Even at an event as choreographed as the Chinese Community Party’s national congress, taking place in Beijing right now, moments of truth slip out.

2012-11-11.title: As Coal Boosts Mozambique, the Rural Poor Are Left Behind
2012-11-11.abstract: Despite an influx of coal projects, poverty rates remain stubbornly high, raising tough questions about whether Africa’s resource boom can effectively raise the standards of living of its people.

2012-11-11.title: In Mozambique, a New Prosperity for Some
2012-11-11.abstract: New gas and coal deals rarely create large numbers of jobs or foster local entrepreneurship.

2012-11-11.title: Amid Calls to Open China’s Politics, Party Digs In
2012-11-11.abstract: A rising chorus of critics say the Communist Party’s agenda is not visionary enough to handle China’s looming crises and set the nation on the path to stability.

2012-11-11.title: The Burdens of Progress
2012-11-11.abstract: As Mozambique receives huge international investments in mining and natural gas, some find jobs and new opportunities while others find displacement and despair.

2012-11-12.title: The Second Term Anti-Role Model: Bush
2012-11-12.abstract: George W. Bush won re-election too, despite being a weakened president. What lessons should Barack Obama take from his still-unpopular predecessor?

2012-11-12.title: What Party Congress? On ‘Singles Day,’ China Said to Surpass U.S. Cyber Sales
2012-11-12.abstract: Nov. 11 was the fourth day of the weeklong 18th Party Congress, but it was also Singles Day, in which a lot more Chinese took part.

2012-11-12.title: Tunisia Battles Over Pulpits, and Revolt’s Legacy
2012-11-12.abstract: In the aftermath of Tunisia’s revolution, a heated competition is on to redefine the nation religiously and politically.

2012-11-12.title: Officials Say F.B.I. Knew of Petraeus Affair in the Summer
2012-11-12.abstract: F.B.I. agents recognized the stakes of any investigation tied to David H. Petraeus, the C.I.A. director who resigned Friday, but were wary of exposing a private affair with no criminal or security implications.

2012-11-12.title: Amid Upheaval, Obama Loses ‘Source of Stability’
2012-11-12.abstract: For Obama administration officials, the searching for a new C.I.A. director comes at a particularly challenging time.

2012-11-13.title: IHT Special Report: Oil & Money
2012-11-13.abstract: Welcome to the future of oil and gas: The U.S. displacing Saudi Arabia as the number one oil producer in the world. Coal displacing oil as the leading fuel. Drilling in the Mediterranean.

2012-11-13.title: Syria: Parents of Missing Journalist Appeal for Help
2012-11-13.abstract: The parents of Austin Tice, a 31-year-old American freelance reporter, said that the Syrian government told them that it had no information about his whereabouts.

2012-11-13.title: Britain: Radical Cleric Wins Deportation Appeal
2012-11-13.abstract: An immigration appeals tribunal ruled that sending a militant Islamic preacher to Jordan to face terrorism charges would violate his right to a fair trial under European human rights law.

2012-11-13.title: Northern Ireland: Group Says It Killed Prison Officer
2012-11-13.abstract: A new group of militant Irish nationalists calling themselves “the I.R.A.” has claimed responsibility for killing a prison officer in Northern Ireland on Nov. 1.

2012-11-13.title: Angry Turkish Secularists Plant Their Flag at Trial
2012-11-13.abstract: In Silivri, a trial of military officers has prompted protests and has raised concerns about the Islamist-leaning government’s commitment to democracy.

2012-11-14.title: Swedish School’s Big Lesson Begins With Dropping Personal Pronouns
2012-11-14.abstract: The Nicolaigarden school in Stockholm tries to free girls and boys from traditional gender roles, starting with replacing “him” or “her” with “friend.”

2012-11-14.title: Chinese Authorities Putting Pressure on Businesses to Help Censor the Web
2012-11-14.abstract: Companies have been told to buy and install hardware to log the traffic of computers, block selected Web sites and connect with local police servers.

2012-11-14.title: Possible Score for Syrian Rebels: Pictures Show Advanced Missile Systems
2012-11-14.abstract: New photographs appear to show Syrian rebels holding at least two new-generation Manpads — shoulder-fired antiaircraft missile systems.

2012-11-14.title: Riots Erupt Across Jordan Over Gas Prices
2012-11-14.abstract: Demonstrators burned tires, smashed traffic lights and blocked roads in an unparalleled show of anger at a government proposal to increase fuel prices.

2012-11-14.title: In a German Town, Court Finds That Mayor Cried Wolf
2012-11-14.abstract: A district court found that Norbert Moosmann, of the Black Forest town of Rickenbach, had staged the throwing of an empty Molotov cocktail into his office.

2012-11-15.title: Along Syrian Border, Lives Defined by War
2012-11-15.abstract: Everyone along the Turkish-Syrian border, it seems, either wants in on the action or just wants it all to go away.

2012-11-15.title: Corruption in Military Poses a Test for China
2012-11-15.abstract: The depth of graft in the People’s Liberation Army will pose a delicate problem for the new leader, Xi Jinping, one that could undermine the Communist Party.

2012-11-15.title: Lessons for U.S. From a Flood-Prone Land
2012-11-15.abstract: The Netherlands has been hailed for its ideas about protecting cities from flooding, but replicating its success would require a reshaping of American approaches.

2012-11-15.title: Hospital Death in Ireland Renews Fight Over Abortion
2012-11-15.abstract: A woman was reportedly denied a potentially lifesaving abortion while she was having a miscarriage.

2012-11-15.title: Pakistan Frees Taliban Prisoners, Renewing Hopes for Peace Talks
2012-11-15.abstract: Pakistan released at least seven senior Afghan Taliban prisoners on Wednesday, a move that was seen as a sign of good faith by the Pakistanis in the slow-moving peace talks process.

2012-11-16.title: Spain: New Rules Limit Evictions
2012-11-16.abstract: The government on Thursday set new rules to limit evictions of homeowners unable to pay their mortgages, responding to mounting public outrage against banks amid a deepening economic crisis.

2012-11-16.title: Uruguay: A Vote for Marijuana
2012-11-16.abstract: Uruguay moved a step closer to becoming the first country to legalize marijuana, with lawmakers introducing a bill that outlines how the drug would be produced, sold and regulated.

2012-11-16.title: China and India Top List of International Students Attending U.S. Colleges
2012-11-16.abstract: China remains the No. 1 country of origin among international students at U.S. colleges, with 194,029 students in 2011-12. India, with 100,270 students, is still second.

2012-11-16.title: Excluded From Xi’s Big Moment
2012-11-16.abstract: The New York Times, the International Herald Tribune and Bloomberg were among news organizations not invited to the presentation of the new Standing Committee of the Politburo. Why?

2012-11-16.title: Global Fund Sees Changes, Not All of Them Welcomed
2012-11-16.abstract: One of the world’s leading health-funding agencies Thursday hired a new director, dismissed its inspector general and announced a new approach to making grants.

2012-11-17.title: For Fashion’s Glitterati, ‘Trade not Aid’ Shoots Through IHT Conference
2012-11-17.abstract: Bono was a highlight of a conference dedicated to Africa, fashion and development, but he was not the only one.

2012-11-17.title: Hague Court Overturns Convictions of 2 Croatian Generals Over a 1995 Offensive
2012-11-17.abstract: A United Nations appeals court on Friday unexpectedly overturned the war crimes convictions of two Croatian generals involved in an offensive that resulted in the death of hundreds of Serbian civilians.

2012-11-17.title: Protesters Come Up Empty in Jordan
2012-11-17.abstract: A wave of demonstrations against King Abdullah II set off by an increase in fuel prices appeared to reach its peak on Friday as thousands of protesters gathered in Amman to call for the monarch’s ouster.

2012-11-17.title: U.S. Fears a Ground War in Gaza Could Hurt Israel and Help Hamas
2012-11-17.abstract: President Obama has supported Israel’s right to defend itself, but administration officials fear that escalating conflict could further damage the country’s fragile standing in the region.

2012-11-17.title: As Battlefield Changes, Israel Takes Tougher Approach
2012-11-17.abstract: Israel acknowledges that its dangerous world is growing more dangerous. For the country and most of its people, that means not concessions but toughness in pursuit of deterrence.

2012-11-18.title: Tensions Grow Between Israel and Hamas
2012-11-18.abstract: Ethan Bronner of The New York Times reports from Tel Aviv on continuing attacks between Israel and Hamas.

2012-11-18.title: With Longer Reach, Rockets Bolster Hamas Arsenal
2012-11-18.abstract: Israel is focused on arms that for the first time have enabled Hamas to strike as far as Tel Aviv and Jerusalem.

2012-11-18.title: Obama’s Visit to Myanmar: Validation, or a ‘Disgrace’?
2012-11-18.abstract: President Obama is due to arrive Monday in Myanmar as part of the ongoing U.S. pivot toward the Asia-Pacific region. His trip also would seem to endorse the democratic reforms under way in Myanmar, although one exiled Burmese activist calls the visit “a disgrace.”

2012-11-18.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-11-18.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.

2012-11-18.title: Israeli Airstrike Kills Three Generations of a Palestinian Family
2012-11-18.abstract: An Israeli bomb pummeled a home deep into the ground in Gaza City, killing 11 people and further indicating that Israel was striking a wider range of targets.

2012-11-19.title: A Look at the Violence in Gaza
2012-11-19.abstract: The New York Times’s Jodi Rudoren reports from Gaza City on an Israeli airstrike that killed several people, including children.

2012-11-19.title: Congo Rebels Advancing on Major City
2012-11-19.abstract: Fighting inched perilously close to Goma on Sunday and United Nations forces turned to heavy aerial bombardments to stop a rebel advance, according to witnesses and officials of Congo’s army.

2012-11-19.title: Asian-American Voters Pivot Toward the Democrats
2012-11-19.abstract: Asian-American voters, who comprised 3 percent of this year’s electorate, went almost three to one for President Barack Obama. As a voting bloc, they are more affluent and better educated than whites.

2012-11-19.title: Israeli Iron Dome Stops a Rocket With a Rocket
2012-11-19.abstract: In the five days since Israel began its assault on the militant infrastructure in Hamas-run Gaza, Iron Dome has successfully intercepted more than 300 rockets, with a success rate of 80 to 90 percent, officials said.

2012-11-19.title: An Outgunned Hamas Tries to Tap Islamists’ Growing Clout
2012-11-19.abstract: As Israel and Hamas met separately with Egyptian officials for indirect talks about a truce in the ongoing Gaza fight, Hamas seemed to be trying to exert its sway over ideological allies in Egypt’s government.

2012-11-20.title: Hamas Dismisses Diplomatic Efforts
2012-11-20.abstract: As Israel conducted a new wave of strikes on Monday, Khaled Meshal, the Hamas leader, suggested the Israeli mobilization on the border was a bluff on the part of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel.

2012-11-20.title: Nov. 19, 2012
2012-11-20.abstract: As Israel conducted a new wave of strikes on Monday, Khaled Meshal, the Hamas leader, suggested that the Israeli mobilization on the border was a bluff on the part of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel.

2012-11-20.title: Activists Use Webcast to Try to Ignite Climate Change Passions
2012-11-20.abstract: The organizers of the 24-hour live Dirty Weather Report reached more than 16 million viewers. Will it help save the planet?

2012-11-20.title: In Southern Israel, Urban Dwellers Learn to Sprint for Bomb Shelters
2012-11-20.abstract: In cities hit by Palestinian rockets, residents have retreated to designated spaces and know to run when air-raid sirens blare.

2012-11-20.title: Murder Case Against a Sheik Tests Senegal’s New President
2012-11-20.abstract: The decision to charge a powerful religious leader in the death of two followers is viewed by many as a sign that President Macky Sall may pursue previously untouchable figures.

2012-11-21.title: Obama, in Cambodia, Sidesteps Ghosts of American Wartime Past
2012-11-21.abstract: President Obama’s visit did not address the tumultuous events of the 1970s, when American warplanes carpet-bombed the impoverished country.

2012-11-21.title: A Mumbai Student Vents on Facebook, and the Police Come Knocking
2012-11-21.abstract: A medical student in India complained on Facebook about the attention a political leader’s death was getting, and she and a friend were charged.

2012-11-21.title: New H.I.V. Cases Falling in Some Poor Nations, but Treatment Still Lags
2012-11-21.abstract: A U.N. report showed that 2.5 million people became infected last year, while only 1.4 million received lifesaving treatment for the first time.

2012-11-21.title: Asian Nations Plan Trade Bloc That, Unlike U.S.’s, Invites China
2012-11-21.abstract: A new 16-nation grouping is seen as a rival to a trade initiative of the Obama administration. The proposal is enthusiastically embraced by China.

2012-11-21.title: Turkey Finds It Is Sidelined as Broker in Mideast
2012-11-21.abstract: Turkey has found itself largely shut out of the central and defining Arab-Israeli conflict in Gaza.

2012-11-22.title: Jordan Protesters Dream of Shift to King’s Brother
2012-11-22.abstract: While an opposition movement begins to press for democratic political change, critics say the notion of a transition to Prince Hamzah is animated in part by nostalgia.

2012-11-22.title: Mountaintop Town Is a Diverse Haven From Syria’s Horrors
2012-11-22.abstract: Residents of Maloula, an oasis of tolerance, have vowed not to succumb to sectarianism and be dragged into the chaos in other parts of the country.

2012-11-22.title: Explosion Deepens Old Fears
2012-11-22.abstract: A bomb went off on a passenger bus on Tuesday, injuring more than 20 people in the heart of Tel Aviv. The blast reminded residents of the fears felt after the second Palestinian uprising.

2012-11-22.title: Helmut Sonnenfeldt, Expert on Soviet and European Affairs, Is Dead at 86
2012-11-22.abstract: Mr. Sonnenfeldt was known as “Kissinger’s Kissinger” for his influence in advising Henry A. Kissinger, the architect of American foreign policy in the Nixon and Ford administrations.

2012-11-22.title: Egyptian President and Obama Forge Link in Gaza Deal
2012-11-22.abstract: The agreement brokered between Israel and Hamas was the start of an unlikely alliance with a fair measure of risk for President Obama and Mohamed Morsi, his counterpart in Egypt.

2012-11-23.title: Endangered and Targeted: Fight to Save Oriental Stork Captivates China
2012-11-23.abstract: In a country where citizens and conservationists alike are used to tales of damage to the environment and wildlife, the story of a massacre of migratory birds has sparked a call for change.

2012-11-23.title: Violence Continues in Congo
2012-11-23.abstract: A battle raged on Thursday for control of an eastern town in the Democratic Republic of Congo that rebels recently seized.

2012-11-23.title: And on Your Left, Behind Those Walls, Lobbyists Are at Work
2012-11-23.abstract: A walking tour in Berlin tries to raise awareness of lobbying in Germany, a country with a low tolerance for money in politics.

2012-11-23.title: Russian Judge Dismisses Suit Against Madonna
2012-11-23.abstract: The singer was sued by antigay groups who contended that during a concert in August she violated a local law against advocating homosexuality.

2012-11-23.title: Russia Won’t Investigate Abduction Claims
2012-11-23.abstract: Leonid Razvozzhayev was abducted in Kiev and returned to Moscow last month, but authorities said they would not pursue the case because Mr. Razvozzhayev had no evidence of the abduction.

2012-11-24.title: New Fatal SARS-Related Cases Reported in Middle East
2012-11-24.abstract: Four new cases were reported by the World Health Organization on Friday. Of the six known cases — all in Saudi Arabia or Qatar — two were fatal.

2012-11-24.title: Russia: Corruption Case Grows
2012-11-24.abstract: A former Defense Ministry official in Russia has been charged with embezzling more than $12 million while working for a private military contractor.

2012-11-24.title: Where ‘In Bed With Media’ Can Be Taken More Literally
2012-11-24.abstract: The election of François Hollande, the first French president from the left in 17 years, has sparked a shuffling of the news media ranks, along with potential conflicts of interest.

2012-11-24.title: Branded a Betrayer for Embracing Syria’s Rebels
2012-11-24.abstract: Samar Yazbek, a Syrian Alawite, has written a chronicle of the uprising’s first months and the pain her participation in it has brought.

2012-11-24.title: China: Nuclear Chief Promoted
2012-11-24.abstract: The new Communist Party chief promoted Wei Fenghe, a member of the military commission and commander of the Second Artillery Corps, to full general.

2012-11-25.title: Vladka Meed, Who Infiltrated Warsaw Ghetto, Dies at 90
2012-11-25.abstract: Mrs. Meed, with her flawless Polish and Aryan good looks, was able to smuggle pistols, gasoline for firebombs and even dynamite to the Jewish fighters inside the Warsaw Ghetto during World War II.

2012-11-25.title: Boris Strugatsky, Writer Who Slyly Criticized Soviets, Dies at 79
2012-11-25.abstract: Mr. Strugatsky collaborated with his brother, Arkady, on science fiction works that mirrored the Soviet Union.

2012-11-25.title: A New Map in Chinese Passports Stirs Anger Across the Region
2012-11-25.abstract: A revised map in China’s new passports shows various disputed territories to be under Chinese sovereignty, from a desolate chunk of the Himalayas to hotly contested islands in the South China Sea. Other countries in the region are not amused.

2012-11-25.title: ‘Gangnam Style’ Becomes Most-Watched Video Ever on YouTube — 805 Million Views
2012-11-25.abstract: “Gangnam Style,” the surprise hit by the South Korean rapper Psy, has replaced Justin Bieber’s “Baby” as the most-watched video of all time on YouTube.

2012-11-25.title: Fatal Fire in Bangladesh Highlights the Dangers Facing Garment Workers
2012-11-25.abstract: At least 111 bodies were recovered from a garment factory fire, and scores of injured were taken to hospitals with burns and smoke inhalation injuries.

2012-11-26.title: Hopes of Home Fade Among Japan’s Displaced
2012-11-26.abstract: With the slow pace of cleanup efforts, residents of Okuma, a town evacuated in the wake of the Fukushima Daiichi disaster, have become pessimistic about ever living there again.

2012-11-26.title: Divisive Election in Spain’s Catalonia Gives Win to Separatist Parties
2012-11-26.abstract: The victory raised the likelihood that Spain’s most powerful economic region will hold an independence referendum that Madrid has vowed to block.

2012-11-26.title: Rebuilding in Gaza and Israel
2012-11-26.abstract: Life is starting to return to normalcy after the eight days of lethal conflict between Israel and Hamas.

2012-11-26.title: Gaza Conflict: Nov. 25, 2012
2012-11-26.abstract: Life is starting to return to normal after eight days of lethal conflict between Israel and Hamas.

2012-11-26.title: As Bombs Fell in Gaza, a Rich Cactus Lover Could Cultivate Only Patience
2012-11-26.abstract: During eight days of bombardment, a builder named Jawdat N. Khoudary could only lie awake at night and cling to his patience, subsisting mostly on cigarettes and clementines.

2012-11-27.title: Venezuelan Judge Who Angered Chávez Says She Was Raped While in Prison
2012-11-27.abstract: In a new book, Judge María Lourdes Afiuni gives her first public account of an allegation from 2010. The government said she was making it up for political purposes.

2012-11-27.title: Beer Lovers Fear an Unequal Tax Bite in Wine Country
2012-11-27.abstract: The French government’s plan to increase the beer tax by 160 percent has upset brewers, beer drinkers and bar owners, who ask why the proposal does not affect winemakers.

2012-11-27.title: Oil Royalties Bill Ignites Protest and Divides Parts of Brazil
2012-11-27.abstract: The legislation, aimed at shifting big portions of royalties from petroleum-rich states along Brazil’s coast, would deal a blow to Rio de Janeiro, the host of the 2014 World Cup.

2012-11-27.title: Audit Says Kabul Bank Began as ‘Ponzi Scheme’
2012-11-27.abstract: The report asserts that Afghanistan’s largest financial institution existed primarily to allow a narrow clique tied to President Hamid Karzai’s government to siphon riches from depositors.

2012-11-27.title: Judge Orders Toronto Mayor to Leave Office
2012-11-27.abstract: The unusual court order relates to $3,150 that Mayor Rob Ford solicited while a city councilor from lobbyists and corporations  for a private children’s football charity.

2012-11-28.title: Ireland’s Abortion Policy to Be Clarified This Year
2012-11-28.abstract: The government said Tuesday that it would clarify its abortion policy by the end of the year after publishing a long-overdue report on an issue that has divided the country

2012-11-28.title: Downing of Copter May Show a New Syrian Rebel Capability
2012-11-28.abstract: Rebels hit a military helicopter with a surface-to-air missile outside Aleppo, a video appeared to show, marking a major battlefield advance.

2012-11-28.title: Sunni Leaders Gaining Clout in Mideast
2012-11-28.abstract: The United States is left somewhat wary about the rising Sunni Muslim alliance of Egypt, Qatar and Turkey and its potential for anti-Western sentiment.

2012-11-28.title: In Germany, Weighing Debt Relief Against Debt Reduction
2012-11-28.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel has preached austerity, even as public spending cuts have deepened recessions in the periphery, actually increasing the debt load.

2012-11-28.title: Kim Jong-un Seems to Get a New Title: Heartthrob
2012-11-28.abstract: It was unclear whether the People’s Daily in China had fallen for an Onion parody naming the North Korean ruler the “Sexiest Man Alive for 2012.”

2012-11-29.title: British Lawmakers Warn Against Press Restrictions
2012-11-29.abstract: Just before release of a major report on the phone hacking scandal, a group of politicians spoke up in defense of press freedom.

2012-11-29.title: Israel Seizes 2 Gaza Boats Near New Offshore Limit
2012-11-29.abstract: Despite an Israeli concession to permit fishing up to six nautical miles from shore, forces detained a Palestinian fisherman as his crew tried to venture farther into the Mediterranean Sea.

2012-11-29.title: European Union Survey Says Outlook for Growth and Jobs Remains ‘Bleak’ for 2013
2012-11-29.abstract: Unemployment will rise for at least another year and the crisis has “severe social consequences” according to a  European Commission report.

2012-11-29.title: Benghazi Violence Is Beyond the Control of Even the City’s Powerful Militias
2012-11-29.abstract: Recent killings, including that of the American ambassador, have brought attention to the lawlessness that has plagued the Libyan city.

2012-11-29.title: U.S. Weighs Bolder Effort to Intervene in Syria’s Conflict
2012-11-29.abstract: The Obama administration, hoping that the war in Syria has reached a turning point, is considering more aggressive action there, including providing arms to some opposition fighters.

2012-11-30.title: The Trouble With ‘Offbeat’ Essays Isn’t the Style, but the Fit
2012-11-30.abstract: Sush Krishnamoorthy, a student at Sardar Patel Vidyalaya, in New Delhi, has eschewed writing about something “fancier” in her college essays for writing about her “ordinary” life.

2012-11-30.title: After Vote, Palestinians and Israel Search for the Next Step
2012-11-30.abstract: Even after Palestine gained recognition as a nonmember state at the United Nations, the two countries seemed stuck in the same stalemate.

2012-11-30.title: Israel’s Antimissile System Attracts Potential Buyers
2012-11-30.abstract: Iron Dome’s most salient feature, according to American experts now examining after-action reports from Gaza, may well be its software.

2012-11-30.title: From the Ashes, Tibetan Buddhism Rises in the Forbidden City
2012-11-30.abstract: As Tibetan self-immolations spread to Chinese provinces, the newly restored Hall of Rectitude, the center of Tibetan Buddhism during China’s last imperial dynasty, is unveiled.

2012-11-30.title: Giving New Life to Vultures to Restore a Human Ritual of Death
2012-11-30.abstract: The Parsi community of Mumbai is awaiting approval on a plan to grow the vulture population and thereby revive a centuries-old death ritual.
